section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports pulling on his ears and experiencing ear pain.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a patient-reported symptom, not an objective exam finding, and belongs in the symptom history rather than the exam section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient is rubbing his nose frequently.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is primarily a behavior described in the history; while it could be observed on exam, it is presented as a patient behavior rather than a discrete physical finding.
Sweating is described as part of the symptom complex in the history; unless explicitly documented as an observed sign, it fits better in HPI.

section_miscategorizations.text: Order shoulder radiographs and initiate physical therapy.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Content is plan-type but is clearly unrelated to the presenting URI/ear/nasal complaint and appears to be an erroneously inserted plan for a different problem, which could mislead a reader about the current visit's focus.

section_miscategorizations.text: Arrange follow-up in four weeks if symptoms persist.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This follow-up timing conflicts with other follow-up instructions in the same plan (one week and six weeks), suggesting misplacement or carryover from another context, though it remains plan-type content.

This follow-up interval is inconsistent with the acute nature of the complaint and other follow-up instructions, suggesting it may belong to a different encounter, but it is still formally a plan element.
